title: Exclusion tracking and subject tracing
text: build cohort three exclusion stages — pattern common clinical trials, epidemiological studies, machine learning pipelines, observational research.
code: lg_start(study_id = "COHORT-001", analysis_id = "main-analysis") #> lineager: session started [study: COHORT-001] [analysis: main-analysis]  # Simulate a patient registry set.seed(42) n <- 20L  raw <- data.frame(   USUBJID = sprintf("PT-%03d", seq_len(n)),   age = sample(15:75, n, replace = TRUE),   sex = sample(c("M", "F"), n, replace = TRUE),   diagnosis = sample(c("Y", "N", "N"), n, replace = TRUE),   consent = sample(c("Y", "Y", "Y", "N"), n, replace = TRUE),   prior_drug = sample(c("Y", "N", "N", "N"), n, replace = TRUE),   biomarker = round(runif(n, 0.5, 8.5), 2),   outcome = ifelse(runif(n) > 0.4, round(rnorm(n, 50, 12), 1), NA_real_),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  registry <- lg_tag(raw,   dataset_id = "REGISTRY",   label = "Patient registry — all screened" ) #> lineager: tagged 'REGISTRY' — 20 rows, 8 cols  cat("Screened: ", nrow(registry), "patients\n") #> Screened:  20 patients

title: Exclusion tracking and subject tracing
text: Every call lg_filter() requires reason. reason becomes canonical documentation exclusion step — appears lg_exclusions(), lg_disposition(), lg_trace(), lg_report().
code: # This would error: # lg_filter(registry, age >= 18L) # Error: A `reason` is required.  # Correct: adults <- lg_filter(registry,   age >= 18L,   reason = "Under minimum age threshold (age < 18 years)" ) #> lineager: [REGISTRY] filter 'Under minimum age threshold (age < 18 years)' — 20 in, 19 out, 1 excluded

title: Exclusion tracking and subject tracing
text: reason_code provides short, controlled-vocabulary label exclusion — useful grouping similar exclusions programmatically.
code: consented <- lg_filter(adults,   consent == "Y",   reason = "Did not provide written informed consent",   reason_code = "NO_CONSENT" ) #> lineager: [REGISTRY] filter 'Did not provide written informed consent' — 19 in, 15 out, 4 excluded  diagnosed <- lg_filter(consented,   diagnosis == "Y",   reason = "Does not meet diagnostic criteria per protocol section 3.1",   reason_code = "NO_DIAGNOSIS" ) #> lineager: [REGISTRY] filter 'Does not meet diagnostic criteria per protocol section 3.1' — 15 in, 2 out, 13 excluded

what: population: grouping exclusions by analysis set
title: Exclusion tracking and subject tracing
text: population groups exclusions named cohorts — corresponding analysis set flags clinical data (SAFFL, ITTFL, etc.) cohort definitions epidemiology.
code:
no_prior <- lg_filter(diagnosed,   prior_drug == "N",   reason      = "Received prohibited prior medication within wash-out period",   reason_code = "PRIOR_MED",   population  = "ELIGIBLE_SET" ) #> lineager: [REGISTRY] filter 'Received prohibited prior medication within wash-out period' — 2 in, 2 out, 0 excluded  biomarker_pos <- lg_filter(no_prior,   biomarker >= 2.0,   reason = "Biomarker below threshold (< 2.0) per protocol section 4.3",   reason_code = "LOW_BIOMARKER",   population = "BIOMARKER_POS" ) #> lineager: [REGISTRY] filter 'Biomarker below threshold (< 2.0) per protocol section 4.3' — 2 in, 2 out, 0 excluded  analysis_set <- lg_filter(biomarker_pos,   !is.na(outcome),   reason      = "Missing primary outcome measurement",   reason_code = "MISSING_OUTCOME",   population  = "ANALYSIS_SET" ) #> lineager: [REGISTRY] filter 'Missing primary outcome measurement' — 2 in, 1 out, 1 excluded  cat("Screened:     ", nrow(registry), "
") #> Screened:      20 cat("Adults:       ", nrow(adults), "
") #> Adults:        19 cat("Consented:    ", nrow(consented), "
") #> Consented:     15 cat("Diagnosed:    ", nrow(diagnosed), "
") #> Diagnosed:     2 cat("No prior med: ", nrow(no_prior), "
") #> No prior med:  2 cat("Biomarker+:   ", nrow(biomarker_pos), "
") #> Biomarker+:    2 cat("Analysis set: ", nrow(analysis_set), "
") #> Analysis set:  1

title: Exclusion tracking and subject tracing
text: trace shows: - tagged datasets contain row - operations (order) touched datasets containing row - exclusion records row, reasons population

result <- lg_trace(excluded_id, verbose = FALSE)  cat("Subject:       ", result$usubjid, "

title: Exclusion tracking and subject tracing
text: lg_operations() returns full sequence operations data frame — useful understanding pipeline structure automating documentation. difference rows_in rows_out number rows excluded operation — matching exclusion records registered step.
code:
ops <- lg_operations() ops[, c(   "op_id", "op_type", "dataset_id", "description",   "rows_in", "rows_out" )] #>     op_id op_type dataset_id #> 1 op_0001  FILTER   REGISTRY #> 2 op_0002  FILTER   REGISTRY #> 3 op_0003  FILTER   REGISTRY #> 4 op_0004  FILTER   REGISTRY #> 5 op_0005  FILTER   REGISTRY #> 6 op_0006  FILTER   REGISTRY #>                                                   description rows_in rows_out #> 1                Under minimum age threshold (age < 18 years)      20       19 #> 2                    Did not provide written informed consent      19       15 #> 3  Does not meet diagnostic criteria per protocol section 3.1      15        2 #> 4 Received prohibited prior medication within wash-out period       2        2 #> 5  Biomarker below threshold (< 2.0) per protocol section 4.3       2        2 #> 6                         Missing primary outcome measurement       2        1 # Verify: total excluded == sum of (rows_in - rows_out) across FILTER ops filter_ops <- ops[ops$op_type == "FILTER", ] total_via_ops <- sum(filter_ops$rows_in - filter_ops$rows_out) total_via_excl <- nrow(lg_exclusions()) #> lineager: 19 exclusion(s) retrieved cat("Excluded via ops:  ", total_via_ops, "
") #> Excluded via ops:   19 cat("Excluded via excl: ", total_via_excl, "
") #> Excluded via excl:  19 cat("Match:             ", total_via_ops == total_via_excl, "
") #> Match:              TRUE

title: Exclusion tracking and subject tracing
text: Continue vignette("populations--reporting") population flag registration report generation.
code:
lg_start() #> lineager: session started  cohort <- lg_tag(   data.frame(     id = sprintf("S%02d", 1:10),     enrolled = c(rep(TRUE, 8), FALSE, FALSE),     treated = c(rep(TRUE, 6), FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE),     complete = c(rep(TRUE, 4), FALSE, FALSE, rep(FALSE, 4)),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   dataset_id = "COHORT" ) #> lineager: tagged 'COHORT' — 10 rows, 4 cols  step1 <- lg_filter(cohort, enrolled == TRUE,   reason = "Not enrolled in study" ) #> lineager: [COHORT] filter 'Not enrolled in study' — 10 in, 8 out, 2 excluded step2 <- lg_filter(step1, treated == TRUE,   reason = "Did not receive study treatment" ) #> lineager: [COHORT] filter 'Did not receive study treatment' — 8 in, 6 out, 2 excluded step3 <- lg_filter(step2, complete == TRUE,   reason = "Did not complete the study" ) #> lineager: [COHORT] filter 'Did not complete the study' — 6 in, 4 out, 2 excluded  cat("Enrolled:  ", nrow(step1), "
") #> Enrolled:   8 cat("Treated:   ", nrow(step2), "
") #> Treated:    6 cat("Completed: ", nrow(step3), "
") #> Completed:  4  lg_disposition(by = "reason") #>                             group n_excluded #> 1      Did not complete the study          2 #> 2 Did not receive study treatment          2 #> 3           Not enrolled in study          2 lg_end() #> lineager: session ended — 3 operation(s), 6 exclusion(s), 0 population(s), 0 var spec(s)

title: Getting started with lineager
text: lg_tag() entry point. assigns .__lid__ (lineage ID) every row position 1. ID persists operations. lineage ID format embeds dataset ID zero-padded sequence. USUBJID column present (CDISC datasets), also embedded human readability:
code: patients <- data.frame(   USUBJID = c("P001", "P002", "P003", "P004", "P005", "P006"),   age = c(34L, 19L, 52L, 28L, 61L, 44L),   group = c("A", "B", "A", "B", "A", "B"),   eligible = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  tagged <- lg_tag(patients,   dataset_id = "PATIENTS",   label = "Patient registry" ) #> lineager: tagged 'PATIENTS' — 6 rows, 4 cols  tagged #> <lg_df> 'PATIENTS'  [6 × 5] #>   USUBJID age group eligible #> 1    P001  34     A     TRUE #> 2    P002  19     B    FALSE #> 3    P003  52     A     TRUE #> 4    P004  28     B     TRUE #> 5    P005  61     A    FALSE #> 6    P006  44     B     TRUE PATIENTS_000001   ← non-CDISC: dataset + sequence DM_0001_01-042    ← CDISC: dataset + sequence + USUBJID

title: Getting started with lineager
text: Tag source datasets transformations:
code: labs <- data.frame(   USUBJID = c("P001", "P001", "P003", "P004", "P006"),   test = c("ALT", "AST", "ALT", "ALT", "ALT"),   value = c(28.4, 31.2, 45.1, 22.8, 38.6),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  labs_tagged <- lg_tag(labs, dataset_id = "LABS", label = "Laboratory results") #> lineager: tagged 'LABS' — 5 rows, 3 cols  cat("Patients tagged:", nrow(tagged), "rows\n") #> Patients tagged: 6 rows cat("Labs tagged:    ", nrow(labs_tagged), "rows\n") #> Labs tagged:     5 rows

title: Getting started with lineager
text: lg_derive() works like dplyr::mutate() requires description argument recorded operation log. .__lid__ column preserved unchanged derivations: Chain derivations naturally: call adds one DERIVE operation session log.
code: derived <- lg_derive(tagged,   age_group = ifelse(age >= 40L, ">=40", "<40"),   adult = age >= 18L,   description = "age_group: >=40 vs <40 from age; adult: age >= 18" ) #> lineager: [PATIENTS] derive — age_group: >=40 vs <40 from age; adult: age >= 18  derived[, c("USUBJID", "age", "age_group", "adult")] #> <lg_df> 'PATIENTS'  [6 × 4] #>   USUBJID age age_group adult #> 1    P001  34       <40  TRUE #> 2    P002  19       <40  TRUE #> 3    P003  52      >=40  TRUE #> 4    P004  28       <40  TRUE #> 5    P005  61      >=40  TRUE #> 6    P006  44      >=40  TRUE all(derived[[".__lid__"]] == tagged[[".__lid__"]]) #> [1] TRUE derived2 <- lg_derive(derived,   label = paste0(USUBJID, " (", group, ")"),   description = "Display label combining USUBJID and group" ) #> lineager: [PATIENTS] derive — Display label combining USUBJID and group  derived2[, c(".__lid__", "USUBJID", "group", "label")] #> <lg_df> 'PATIENTS'  [6 × 4] #>   USUBJID group    label #> 1    P001     A P001 (A) #> 2    P002     B P002 (B) #> 3    P003     A P003 (A) #> 4    P004     B P004 (B) #> 5    P005     A P005 (A) #> 6    P006     B P006 (B)

title: Getting started with lineager
text: lg_join() performs tracked join. preserves .__lid__ left dataset adds .__lid_y__ record rows right dataset contributed — enabling bilateral tracing. .__lid_y__ column shows LABS row contributed PATIENTS row. Rows matching lab record NA .__lid_y__. Supported join types:
code: joined <- lg_join(tagged, labs_tagged,   by          = "USUBJID",   type        = "left",   description = "Merge ALT lab values from LABS onto PATIENTS" ) #> lineager: [PATIENTS + LABS] left join — 7 rows out  joined[, c(".__lid__", "USUBJID", "eligible", "test", "value", ".__lid_y__")] #> <lg_df> 'PATIENTS'  [7 × 6] #>   USUBJID eligible test value     .__lid_y__ #> 1    P001     TRUE  ALT  28.4 LABS_0001_P001 #> 2    P001     TRUE  AST  31.2 LABS_0002_P001 #> 3    P002    FALSE <NA>    NA           <NA> #> 4    P003     TRUE  ALT  45.1 LABS_0003_P003 #> 5    P004     TRUE  ALT  22.8 LABS_0004_P004 #> 6    P005    FALSE <NA>    NA           <NA> #> # … 1 more rows lg_join(x, y, by = "USUBJID", type = "left") # all rows of x lg_join(x, y, by = "USUBJID", type = "inner") # only matching rows lg_join(x, y, by = "USUBJID", type = "full") # all rows of both lg_join(x, y, by = "USUBJID", type = "right") # all rows of y

title: Getting started with lineager
text: lg_filter() works like dplyr::filter() reason argument mandatory default. Every row removal must documented. Excluded rows IDs captured session exclusion registry automatically. Optional arguments enrich exclusion record:
code:
eligible_only <- lg_filter(tagged,   eligible == TRUE,   reason = "Not eligible for analysis (eligible != TRUE)" ) #> lineager: [PATIENTS] filter 'Not eligible for analysis (eligible != TRUE)' — 6 in, 4 out, 2 excluded  cat("Before:", nrow(tagged), "
") #> Before: 6 cat("After: ", nrow(eligible_only), "
") #> After:  4 # reason_code and population enrich the exclusion record step1 <- lg_filter(tagged,   eligible == TRUE,   reason = "Screening criteria not met (eligible != TRUE)",   reason_code = "SCREEN_FAIL",   population = "ELIGIBLE_SET" ) #> lineager: [PATIENTS] filter 'Screening criteria not met (eligible != TRUE)' — 6 in, 4 out, 2 excluded  step2 <- lg_filter(step1,   age >= 18L,   reason = "Under minimum age threshold (age < 18)",   reason_code = "UNDERAGE",   population = "ADULT_SET" ) #> lineager: [PATIENTS] filter 'Under minimum age threshold (age < 18)' — 4 in, 4 out, 0 excluded  cat("Enrolled: ", nrow(tagged), "
") #> Enrolled:  6 cat("Eligible: ", nrow(step1), "
") #> Eligible:  4 cat("Adult:    ", nrow(step2), "
") #> Adult:     4

title: Getting started with lineager
text: Every lg_derive(), lg_join(), lg_filter() call adds entry session operation log. Retrieve lg_operations(): operation log backbone provenance report — shows complete sequence transformations applied data.
code: ops <- lg_operations() ops[, c("op_id", "op_type", "description", "rows_in", "rows_out")] #>     op_id   op_type                                       description rows_in #> 1 op_0001    DERIVE age_group: >=40 vs <40 from age; adult: age >= 18       6 #> 2 op_0002    DERIVE         Display label combining USUBJID and group       6 #> 3 op_0003 JOIN_LEFT      Merge ALT lab values from LABS onto PATIENTS       6 #> 4 op_0004    FILTER      Not eligible for analysis (eligible != TRUE)       6 #> 5 op_0005    FILTER     Screening criteria not met (eligible != TRUE)       6 #> 6 op_0006    FILTER            Under minimum age threshold (age < 18)       4 #>   rows_out #> 1        6 #> 2        6 #> 3        7 #> 4        4 #> 5        4 #> 6        4

text: lg_end() marks session inactive prints summary. store remains memory still queryable via lg_trace(), lg_exclusions(), etc. lg_start() called .
code: lg_end() #> lineager: session ended — 6 operation(s), 4 exclusion(s), 0 population(s), 0 var spec(s)

title: Getting started with lineager
text: lg_lineage() builds complete lineage graph pipeline — every dataset, operation, exclusion branch — returns Graphviz DOT string. Render inline export file. Node colour legend: next two vignettes cover exclusion tracking reporting depth: vignette("exclusion-tracking") — lg_filter(), lg_exclusions(), lg_disposition(), lg_trace() detailed examples vignette("populations--reporting") — lg_population(), lg_spec(), lg_report(), lg_lineage() / lg_plot() structured documentation pipeline visualisation

lg_start() #> lineager: session started raw <- lg_tag(   data.frame(     USUBJID = sprintf("P%02d", 1:6),     group = rep(c("A", "B"), 3L),     flag = c(TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   dataset_id = "RAW" ) #> lineager: tagged 'RAW' — 6 rows, 3 cols raw <- lg_derive(raw,   group_n = ifelse(group == "A", 1L, 2L),   description = "Numeric group code" ) #> lineager: [RAW] derive — Numeric group code lg_filter(raw, flag == TRUE, reason = "Flag not set") #> lineager: [RAW] filter 'Flag not set' — 6 in, 4 out, 2 excluded #> <lg_df> 'RAW'  [4 × 5] #>   USUBJID group flag group_n #> 1     P01     A TRUE       1 #> 2     P02     B TRUE       2 #> 3     P04     B TRUE       2 #> 4     P06     B TRUE       2  lin <- lg_lineage() print(lin) #> <lg_lineage>  1 source dataset(s), 2 operation(s), 1 exclusion branch(es) #> Use lg_plot(lin) to render. title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
text: Every ADaM derivation involves exclusions — screen failures, protocol deviations, missing baselines, assessment windows. trials documented aggregate: “47 subjects excluded per-protocol population.” regulators increasingly ask : rows? ? can trace subject complete derivation pipeline? lineager answers questions row level. Every row every dataset carries lineage ID (.__lid__) survives filters, joins, derivations. Every lg_filter() call requires documented reason, session automatically accumulates exclusion registry pipeline runs — manual disposition tracking needed. article builds ADEFF dataset Phase III atopic dermatitis trial — starting raw ADSL ADLB, deriving baseline change baseline, tracking every exclusion, producing CDISC Reviewer’s Guide-aligned provenance report.

title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
code: library(lineager) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
text: simulate CDISC ADaM-structured ADSL (subject-level) ADLB (lab/ assessment) datasets Phase III trial dupilumab moderate--severe atopic dermatitis. 320 randomised subjects, primary endpoint EASI score.
code: n_total <- 320L  # ── ADSL ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── adsl_raw <- data.frame(   STUDYID  = "DERM-DUP-301",   USUBJID  = sprintf("DUP301-%04d", seq_len(n_total)),   TRT01P   = rep(c("Dupilumab 300mg Q2W", "Placebo Q2W"), each = n_total / 2),   AGE      = round(rnorm(n_total, mean = 36, sd = 14)),   SEX      = sample(c("M", "F"), n_total, replace = TRUE),   ITTFL    = ifelse(runif(n_total) < 0.97, "Y", "N"),   SAFFL    = ifelse(runif(n_total) < 0.98, "Y", "N"),   PPROTFL  = ifelse(runif(n_total) < 0.91, "Y", "N"),   EASISCAT = sample(c("MODERATE", "SEVERE"), n_total, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.55, 0.45)),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # ── ADLB: EASI scores at baseline and Week 16 ────────────────────────────── adlb_raw <- expand.grid(   USUBJID = adsl_raw$USUBJID,   AVISITN = c(0L, 16L),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) |>   merge(adsl_raw[, c("USUBJID", "TRT01P", "EASISCAT")], by = "USUBJID") |>   arrange(USUBJID, AVISITN)  set.seed(2026) adlb_raw$AVAL <- mapply(function(trt, vis, scat) {   base_mean <- if (scat == "SEVERE") 45 else 30   if (vis == 0L) return(round(pmax(0, rnorm(1, base_mean, 8)), 1))   reduction <- if (trt == "Dupilumab 300mg Q2W") 0.55 else 0.12   round(pmax(0, base_mean * (1 - reduction) + rnorm(1, 0, 5)), 1) }, adlb_raw$TRT01P, adlb_raw$AVISITN, adlb_raw$EASISCAT)  # Missingness at Week 16 (~6%) post_bl <- adlb_raw$AVISITN > 0 miss_idx <- which(post_bl)[sample(sum(post_bl), round(sum(post_bl) * 0.06))] adlb_raw$AVAL[miss_idx] <- NA_real_  adlb_raw$PARAMCD <- "EASI"  cat(sprintf(   "ADSL: %d subjects | ADLB: %d records\n", nrow(adsl_raw), nrow(adlb_raw) )) #> ADSL: 320 subjects | ADLB: 640 records

title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
text: lg_start() initialises session store. lg_* functions write store automatically.
code: lg_start(study_id = "DERM-DUP-301", analysis_id = "ADEFF-primary-efficacy") #> lineager: session started [study: DERM-DUP-301] [analysis: ADEFF-primary-efficacy]

title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
text: lg_tag() assigns unique lineage ID (.__lid__) every row. USUBJID embedded automatically present, making IDs human-readable.
code: adsl <- lg_tag(adsl_raw, dataset_id = "ADSL", domain = "DM", label = "Subject-Level") #> lineager: tagged 'ADSL' — 320 rows, 9 cols adlb <- lg_tag(adlb_raw, dataset_id = "ADLB", domain = "LB", label = "Laboratory/Efficacy") #> lineager: tagged 'ADLB' — 640 rows, 6 cols head(adsl[, c(".__lid__", "USUBJID", "TRT01P", "ITTFL")], 4L) #> <lg_df> 'ADSL' (domain: DM)  [4 × 4] #>       USUBJID              TRT01P ITTFL #> 1 DUP301-0001 Dupilumab 300mg Q2W     Y #> 2 DUP301-0002 Dupilumab 300mg Q2W     Y #> 3 DUP301-0003 Dupilumab 300mg Q2W     Y #> 4 DUP301-0004 Dupilumab 300mg Q2W     Y

what: Step 1: Document and apply the safety population
title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
text: lg_population() registers population flag definition — inclusion criteria, exclusion criteria, plain-English description — directly linking flag already present data.
code: lg_population(   adsl,   flag_var      = "SAFFL",   label         = "Safety Analysis Flag",   definition    = "All randomised subjects who received at least one dose of study treatment",   incl_criteria = c("Randomised", "Received >= 1 dose of study treatment"),   excl_criteria = "No study drug administered" ) #> lineager: population 'SAFFL' (Safety Analysis Flag) — 314 included, 6 excluded adsl_saf <- lg_filter(   adsl, SAFFL == "Y",   reason     = "Restrict to safety analysis set (SAFFL = Y) per SAP Section 4.1",   population = "SAFFL" ) #> lineager: [ADSL] filter 'Restrict to safety analysis set (SAFFL = Y) per SAP Section 4.1' — 320 in, 314 out, 6 excluded

title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
code: adsl_itt <- lg_filter(   adsl_saf, ITTFL == "Y",   reason     = "Restrict to ITT population (ITTFL = Y) per SAP Section 4.2",   population = "ITTFL" ) #> lineager: [ADSL] filter 'Restrict to ITT population (ITTFL = Y) per SAP Section 4.2' — 314 in, 304 out, 10 excluded cat(sprintf("After ITT filter: %d subjects\n", nrow(adsl_itt))) #> After ITT filter: 304 subjects

what: Step 3: Join EASI assessments to ADSL covariates
title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
code: adeff <- lg_join(   adlb, adsl_itt[, c("USUBJID", "TRT01P", "EASISCAT")],   by          = "USUBJID",   type        = "inner",   description = "Join ADLB EASI assessments to ITT-restricted ADSL covariates" ) #> lineager: [ADLB + ADSL] inner join — 608 rows out cat(sprintf("After join: %d records\n", nrow(adeff))) #> After join: 608 records

what: Step 4: Derive baseline and change from baseline
title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
code: adeff <- adeff |>   group_by(USUBJID) |>   mutate(BASE = AVAL[AVISITN == 0L][1L]) |>   ungroup()  # Re-tag after the dplyr grouping pipeline to keep lg_df class intact # Drop any existing .__lid__ before re-tagging to avoid duplicate columns adeff_df <- as.data.frame(adeff) adeff_df <- adeff_df[, !names(adeff_df) %in% ".__lid__"] adeff <- lg_tag(adeff_df, dataset_id = "ADEFF", domain = "ADLB",                 label = "Efficacy Analysis Dataset") #> lineager: tagged 'ADEFF' — 608 rows, 9 cols n_miss_base <- sum(is.na(adeff$BASE[adeff$AVISITN == 0L]))  adeff <- lg_filter(   adeff, !is.na(BASE),   reason = "Exclude records with missing baseline EASI score per SAP Section 5.1",   population = "ITTFL" ) #> lineager: [ADEFF] filter 'Exclude records with missing baseline EASI score per SAP Section 5.1' — 608 in, 608 out, 0 excluded adeff <- lg_derive(   adeff,   CHG  = AVAL - BASE,   PCHG = (AVAL - BASE) / BASE * 100,   description = "Derive change from baseline (CHG) and percent change (PCHG) per CDISC ADaM IG Section 3.2.7" ) #> lineager: [ADEFF] derive — Derive change from baseline (CHG) and percent change (PCHG) per CDISC ADaM IG Section 3.2.7

title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
code: adeff_w16 <- lg_filter(   adeff, AVISITN == 16L, !is.na(AVAL),   reason     = "Restrict to Week 16 primary timepoint with non-missing EASI assessment",   population = "ITTFL" ) #> lineager: [ADEFF] filter 'Restrict to Week 16 primary timepoint with non-missing EASI assessment' — 608 in, 285 out, 323 excluded cat(sprintf("Week 16 primary analysis records: %d\n", nrow(adeff_w16))) #> Week 16 primary analysis records: 285

title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
code: adeff_final <- lg_derive(   adeff_w16,   EASI75FL = ifelse(PCHG <= -75, "Y", "N"),   description = "Derive EASI-75 responder flag: Y if percent change <= -75% per SAP Section 5.3" ) #> lineager: [ADEFF] derive — Derive EASI-75 responder flag: Y if percent change <= -75% per SAP Section 5.3

title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
text: lg_spec() records structured derivation specification linking ADaM variable back SDTM source — basis CDISC Reviewer’s Guide derivation section.
code: lg_spec(   adam_dataset  = "ADEFF",   adam_var      = "CHG",   label         = "Change from Baseline EASI Score",   source_domain = "LB",   source_var    = "LBSTRESN",   derivation    = "AVAL - BASE, where BASE is the Visit 0 (Baseline) EASI assessment",   conditions    = "Subjects with non-missing baseline EASI score" )

title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
text: lg_disposition() summarises exclusion registry automatically — manual group definitions needed. Every lg_filter() call contributed exclusions summary.
code: lg_disposition(by = "reason") #>                                                                    group #> 1 Restrict to Week 16 primary timepoint with non-missing EASI assessment #> 2             Restrict to ITT population (ITTFL = Y) per SAP Section 4.2 #> 3        Restrict to safety analysis set (SAFFL = Y) per SAP Section 4.1 #>   n_excluded #> 1        323 #> 2         10 #> 3          6

title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
text: ADEFF derivation exclusion registry — DERM-DUP-301
code: excl <- lg_exclusions() #> lineager: 339 exclusion(s) retrieved excl |>   select(dataset_id, reason, population) |>   knitr::kable(caption = "ADEFF derivation exclusion registry — DERM-DUP-301")

what: Trace a subject through the pipeline
title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented
text: lg_trace() returns complete provenance history subject — every dataset appeared , every operation touched , exclusion records.
code: example_subj <- adeff_final$USUBJID[1L] lg_trace(example_subj) #>  #> ── lineager trace: USUBJID 'DUP301-0001' ── #>  #>   Appears in: ADSL, ADLB, ADEFF #>  #>   Operations: #>     [FILTER] ADSL: Restrict to safety analysis set (SAFFL = Y) per SAP Section  (320→314) #>     [FILTER] ADSL: Restrict to ITT population (ITTFL = Y) per SAP Section 4.2 (314→304) #>     [JOIN_INNER] ADLB: Join ADLB EASI assessments to ITT-restricted ADSL covariates (640→608) #>     [FILTER] ADEFF: Exclude records with missing baseline EASI score per SAP Sec (608→608) #>     [DERIVE] ADEFF: Derive change from baseline (CHG) and percent change (PCHG)  (608→608) #>     [FILTER] ADEFF: Restrict to Week 16 primary timepoint with non-missing EASI  (608→285) #>     [DERIVE] ADEFF: Derive EASI-75 responder flag: Y if percent change <= -75% p (285→285) #>  #>   Exclusions (1): #>     ✗ [ADEFF] Restrict to Week 16 primary timepoint with non-missing EASI assessment [pop: ITTFL] #>  #>   Registered populations: #>     SAFFL: Safety Analysis Flag excluded_subj <- adsl_raw$USUBJID[adsl_raw$ITTFL == "N"][1L] if (!is.na(excluded_subj)) lg_trace(excluded_subj) #>  #> ── lineager trace: USUBJID 'DUP301-0021' ── #>  #>   Appears in: ADSL, ADLB #>  #>   Operations: #>     [FILTER] ADSL: Restrict to safety analysis set (SAFFL = Y) per SAP Section  (320→314) #>     [FILTER] ADSL: Restrict to ITT population (ITTFL = Y) per SAP Section 4.2 (314→304) #>     [JOIN_INNER] ADLB: Join ADLB EASI assessments to ITT-restricted ADSL covariates (640→608) #>  #>   Exclusions (1): #>     ✗ [ADSL] Restrict to ITT population (ITTFL = Y) per SAP Section 4.2 [pop: ITTFL] #>  #>   Registered populations: #>     SAFFL: Safety Analysis Flag

title: Traceable ADaM derivation: every exclusion documented

title: Row-level provenance in real-world evidence studies
text: Real-world evidence studies live die cohort definition. new-user design, washout periods, lookback windows, prevalent user exclusions, outcome ascertainment rules critical analytical decisions HEOR reviewers, payers, regulators scrutinise. Yet RWE studies, documentation decisions aggregate level: CONSORT-style flow chart total numbers step. lineager brings row-level traceability RWE cohort derivation. Every patient record carries lineage ID. Every lg_filter() call requires documented reason, session accumulates exclusion registry automatically pipeline runs. patient can traced complete cohort derivation — raw claims final analysis cohort — time. article demonstrates complete new-user cohort derivation retrospective comparative effectiveness study two diabetes medications administrative claims data.

text: lg_exclusions() returns row-level exclusion registry. lg_operations() returns operation-level log rows_in/rows_out step — right source attrition waterfall. Cohort derivation exclusion registry — T2DM-GLP1-RWE-001
code: excl <- lg_exclusions() #> lineager: 20295 exclusion(s) retrieved excl |>   count(population, reason) |>   knitr::kable(     col.names = c("Population gate", "Reason", "N excluded"),     caption   = "Cohort derivation exclusion registry — T2DM-GLP1-RWE-001"   ) ops <- lg_operations() |> filter(op_type == "FILTER")  waterfall <- data.frame(   label = c("Claims pool", ops$description),   n     = c(n_pool, ops$rows_out),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) waterfall$label_short <- c(   "Claims pool", "Age 40-89", "T2DM diagnosis", "12m lookback",   "New user", "No insulin", "No ESRD", "12m follow-up" ) waterfall$step_num <- seq_len(nrow(waterfall))  ggplot(waterfall, aes(x = reorder(label_short, -step_num), y = n)) +   geom_col(fill = "#1a56db", alpha = 0.8, width = 0.65) +   geom_text(aes(label = scales::comma(n)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3.2, colour = "#0f1117") +   coord_flip() +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))) +   labs(     title    = "Cohort Derivation Attrition",     subtitle = "T2DM-GLP1-RWE-001 | New-user active comparator design",     x = NULL, y = "Patients remaining"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

what: Why row-level provenance matters in RWE
title: Row-level provenance in real-world evidence studies
text: Aggregate exclusion flow charts table stakes. Row-level provenance distinguishes reproducible RWE study defensible one: replication: another analyst can independently derive cohort verify patients included/excluded step — just counts. regulatory review: NICE, ICER, FDA RWE reviewers increasingly ask evidence operational cohort definition matches pre-specified SAP. Row-level traceability makes verifiable, just assertable. sensitivity analyses: re-running derivation alternative lookback window relaxed eligibility criterion, lg_trace() shows exactly patients changed status . audit: provenance report self-contained HTML document recording complete derivation history — without requiring access original code data environment.

DOSEFL: enrolled AND received study treatment" ) #> lineager: [PATIENTS] derive — ENRLFL: enrolled; DOSEFL: enrolled AND received study treatment  # Join lab measurements pts_labs <- lg_join(pts, labs_tagged,   by          = "USUBJID",   type        = "left",   description = "Merge baseline and endpoint lab measurements from LABS" ) #> lineager: [PATIENTS + LABS] left join — 15 rows out  # Derive analysis variables analysis_ds <- lg_derive(pts_labs,   CHG = endpoint - baseline,   PCHG = round((endpoint - baseline) / baseline * 100, 2),   description = paste(     "CHG: absolute change from baseline (endpoint - baseline);",     "PCHG: percent change from baseline"   ) ) #> lineager: [PATIENTS] derive — CHG: absolute change from baseline (endpoint - baseline); PCHG: percent change from baseline  # Filter to enrolled, adult, dosed patients with complete endpoint final <- analysis_ds |>   lg_filter(enrolled == TRUE, adult == TRUE,     reason      = "Not enrolled or under 18",     reason_code = "NOT_ENROLLED"   ) |>   lg_filter(dosed == TRUE,     reason      = "Did not receive study treatment",     reason_code = "NOT_DOSED",     population  = "SAFETY_SET"   ) |>   lg_filter(!is.na(endpoint),     reason      = "Missing primary endpoint measurement",     reason_code = "MISSING_EP",     population  = "ANALYSIS_SET"   ) #> lineager: [PATIENTS] filter 'Not enrolled or under 18' — 15 in, 12 out, 3 excluded #> lineager: [PATIENTS] filter 'Did not receive study treatment' — 12 in, 11 out, 1 excluded #> lineager: [PATIENTS] filter 'Missing primary endpoint measurement' — 11 in, 9 out, 2 excluded  cat("Final analysis set:", nrow(final), "patients\n") #> Final analysis set: 9 patients

what: Row-Level Data Provenance and Exclusion Tracking
title: Row-Level Data Provenance and Exclusion Tracking
text: Row-Level Data Provenance Exclusion Tracking build analysis dataset. filter , join , derive new variables, produce results. Somewhere along way rows disappear. Later, someone asks: “records excluded, , happened record 01-042 source analysis?” Without lineager, answer requires manual reconstruction. lineager, single function call. lineager tags every row every dataset unique lineage identifier survives filters, joins, derivations. Every row removal must carry documented reason. point, lg_trace() returns row’s complete journey across entire pipeline. lg_lineage() visualises full pipeline graph. lg_report() compiles everything structured provenance document. lineager general-purpose: clinical data, machine learning, financial modelling, epidemiology — pipeline row-level accountability matters. CDISC-specific features (domain codes, population flags, SDTM--ADaM mapping, Reviewer’s Guide output) available optional enrichment pharmaceutical users.

title: Row-Level Data Provenance and Exclusion Tracking
code: # Install from GitHub pak::pak("repro-stats/lineager")

title: Row-Level Data Provenance and Exclusion Tracking
code: library(lineager)  # Start a tracked session lg_start(study_id = "TRIAL-001", analysis_id = "primary")  # Use any data frame — here we simulate a small ADSL adsl_raw <- data.frame(   USUBJID = c("01-001", "01-002", "01-003", "01-004", "01-005"),   ARMCD   = c("TRT", "TRT", "SCRNFAIL", "TRT", "TRT"),   EXOCCUR = c("Y", "Y", "N", "Y", "N"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Tag the dataset — every row gets a unique lineage identifier adsl <- lg_tag(adsl_raw, dataset_id = "ADSL", domain = "DM",                label = "Subject-Level Analysis Dataset")  # Derive variables with a documented reason adsl <- lg_derive(adsl,   RANDFL = ifelse(ARMCD != "SCRNFAIL", "Y", "N"),   SAFFL  = ifelse(ARMCD != "SCRNFAIL" & EXOCCUR == "Y", "Y", "N"),   description = "RANDFL: not a screen failure. SAFFL: randomised and dosed." )  # Register the population definition before filtering lg_population(   adsl,   flag_var      = "SAFFL",   label         = "Safety Analysis Set",   definition    = "Randomised subjects who received at least one dose",   incl_criteria = c("Randomised (ARMCD != SCRNFAIL)", "Dosed (EXOCCUR = Y)") )  # Filter with a mandatory exclusion reason — no silent row drops adsl_safety <- lg_filter(   adsl,   SAFFL == "Y",   reason      = "Not in safety population (SAFFL != 'Y')",   reason_code = "NOT_SAFETY",   population  = "SAFFL" )  # Document a source-to-analysis variable derivation spec lg_spec(   dataset_id  = "ADSL",   variable    = "SAFFL",   label       = "Safety Analysis Set Flag",   source_dom  = "DM",   source_var  = "ARMCD",   derivation  = "Y if ARMCD != 'SCRNFAIL' and EXOCCUR = Y, else N" )  # Trace any subject's complete journey across the pipeline lg_trace(adsl_safety$USUBJID[1L])  # Exclusion registry and disposition table lg_exclusions() lg_disposition(by = "reason")  # Visualise the full pipeline graph # Requires DiagrammeR: install.packages("DiagrammeR") # Without it, lg_plot() writes a .dot file you can render externally lin <- lg_lineage() lg_plot(lin)  # Generate a structured provenance report lg_report(   output  = tempfile(fileext = ".html"),   title   = "Data Provenance Report",   sponsor = "Sponsor name",   author  = "Your name" )  lg_end()

what: lineager: Row-Level Data Provenance and Exclusion Tracking — lineager-package
title: lineager: Row-Level Data Provenance and Exclusion Tracking — lineager-package
text: build dataset. filter , join , derive new variables, produce analysis. Somewhere along way rows disappear : subjects excluded, records removed, observations dropped. Later, someone asks: "Show exactly records excluded, , happened record 01-042 source data analysis." lineager makes question answerable : programmatically, existing R pipeline, post-hoc documentation. tags every row every dataset unique lineage identifier survives filters, joins, derivations. Every row removal must carry documented reason. Variable derivations registered structured specifications. point, lg_trace() returns row's complete journey across entire pipeline : source final analysis dataset. lineager general-purpose. works R pipeline row-level provenance matters: clinical data, machine learning, financial modelling, epidemiology, analytical workflow "excluded " question need answer. CDISC-specific features (domain codes, population flags, SDTM--ADaM variable mapping, Reviewer's Guide output) available optional enrichment pharmaceutical clinical users.

title: lineager: Row-Level Data Provenance and Exclusion Tracking — lineager-package
text: Step 1 : Start session tag source data   Step 2 : Derive variables documented descriptions   Step 3 : Filter mandatory exclusion reasons   Step 4 : Trace row generate provenance report
code: lg_start(study_id = "TRIAL-001", analysis_id = "primary-efficacy")  # CDISC datasets dm <- lg_tag(haven::read_sas("sdtm/dm.sas7bdat"),              dataset_id = "DM", domain = "DM",              label = "Demographics")  # General-purpose datasets patients <- lg_tag(patient_df, dataset_id = "patients",                    label = "Patient registry") adsl <- lg_derive(dm,   RANDFL = ifelse(ARMCD != "SCRNFAIL", "Y", "N"),   description = "RANDFL: Y if subject was randomised (ARMCD != 'SCRNFAIL')" )  lg_spec("ADSL", "RANDFL", "Randomised Flag",         source_domain = "DM", source_var = "ARMCD",         derivation    = "Y if ARMCD != 'SCRNFAIL'") adsl_safety <- lg_filter(   adsl,   SAFFL == "Y",   reason      = "Not in safety population (SAFFL != 'Y')",   reason_code = "NOT_SAFETY",   population  = "SAFFL" ) lg_trace("01-042")  lg_report(   output  = "outputs/provenance_report.html",   title   = "Data Provenance Report",   sponsor = "Example Pharma Ltd",   author  = "J. Smith, Biostatistician" )

title: lineager 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: lineager 0.1.0
text: lg_start() / lg_end() — session lifecycle. lg_start() initialises clean session store; lg_end() prints summary marks session inactive. store remains queryable next lg_start(). lg_tag() — entry point. Assigns unique lineage ID (.__lid__) every row. datasets USUBJID column, ID embeds subject identifier human readability (DM_0001_01-042). general datasets, zero-padded sequence used (patients_000001). lg_filter() — tracked filter mandatory reason. Every row removal documented captured session exclusion registry. Optional reason_code population arguments enrich record. lg_derive() — tracked dplyr::mutate() mandatory description. Every variable derivation recorded operation log. lg_join() — tracked join (left, inner, full, right). Preserves .__lid__ x; adds .__lid_y__ record rows y contributed, enabling bilateral tracing.

title: lineager 0.1.0
text: lg_population() — register population cohort flag definition (SAFFL, ITTFL, PPROTFL, custom flag). Records inclusion/exclusion criteria, plain-English definition, automatic counts. lg_spec() — document source--analysis variable derivation. Links output variables back source variables datasets.

title: lineager 0.1.0
text: lg_trace() — trace row’s complete lineage journey: datasets appears , operations passed , exclusion records documented reasons. lg_exclusions() — retrieve full exclusion registry data.frame, filterable population dataset. lg_disposition() — CONSORT-style subject disposition summary, grouped reason, population, dataset. lg_operations() — full pipeline operation log data.frame.

title: lineager 0.1.0
text: lg_lineage() — build lineage graph complete pipeline: source datasets, derive/join/filter operations, exclusion branches, Graphviz DOT string. lg_plot() — render lineage graph inline (via DiagrammeR installed) write DOT source file.

title: lineager 0.1.0
text: lg_report() — generate self-contained HTML provenance report covering dataset inventory, subject disposition, population flag definitions, variable derivations, operation log, exclusion listing. CDISC users, output aligns CDISC Reviewer’s Guide content requirements.
